name: Colour Dilution Alopecia
description: Colour dilution alopecia is a hereditary skin condition associated with the dilute coat colour gene (dd). Abnormal melanin distribution in hair shafts leads to progressive hair loss and recurrent folliculitis in blue or fawn-coloured dogs.
treatment_overview: There is no cure. Regular medicated shampoos, moisturisers, and antibiotics for secondary infections manage the condition. Retinoids have been used with variable success.
prevention_tips: Avoid breeding blue/fawn dogs that develop alopecia
